item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: doing alright?? i haven't read this scp yet so i have no context to this absolutely panicked comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No.

No.

No.

No.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: D-Class aren't all violent criminals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this and think this is pretty good but I have one nitpick, it says after incident 2713-7 they sent in a D-Class, but how’d he get in if “… entry will be refused if the individual's record indicates involvement in any violent criminal incidents…”? Also it says”However, after various incidents the amount of identification required for entry increased, with the highest increase in restrictions occurring after incident 2713-7.” so the D-Class definitely wouldn’t have been granted entry.

item.title: I'm an SCP!
item.content:encoded: My name is Darius, and I rarely see my name anywhere else in media. I can think of a baseball player, a WoW character, and MAYBE passing mention of a couple people over the years. Seeing my name in an SCP article was a nice jolt :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel a cross-over tale between this and the guy who "pushes" the sun around would be an interesting opportunity.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got two links that I feel will work here, and the fact that both can be in-line means they are pretty non-invasive to what I think is a really swell SCP. First off, the GOC mention can be linked to their hub. Second, Cimmerian wrote a very humorous tale featuring this scp object and Dr. Foster, entitled The North Pole, which can be linked to from Dr. Foster's name. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Current knowledge of other ingress points is limited, with the GOC's entry point in Norway being the only other currently known. It is suspected that other groups of interest may also be in possession of ingress points. However, attempts to apprehend individuals associated with other GOIs have found little success due to the interference of SCP-2713-1 instances.

To this:

Current knowledge of other ingress points is limited, with the GOC's entry point in Norway being the only other currently known. It is suspected that other groups of interest may also be in possession of ingress points. However, attempts to apprehend individuals associated with other GOIs have found little success due to the interference of SCP-2713-1 instances.

Changing this:

Following several minutes of exploration, an SCP-2713-2 instance, who self identified as "Malina" invited D-11218 to a back room. D-11218 refused orders from the supervising researcher, Doctor Foster, and complied with the request from the SCP-2713-2 instance. Afterwards, the SCP-2713-2 instance requested to speak directly to Doctor Foster (identifying him by name) which was granted. The following is a log of that conversation.

To this:

Following several minutes of exploration, an SCP-2713-2 instance, who self identified as "Malina" invited D-11218 to a back room. D-11218 refused orders from the supervising researcher, Doctor Foster, and complied with the request from the SCP-2713-2 instance. Afterwards, the SCP-2713-2 instance requested to speak directly to Doctor Foster (identifying him by name) which was granted. The following is a log of that conversation.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's the Moon's fault, isn't it? The sick bastard!

Seriously, Inupiat lore…

Alignaq, Malina's brother, the moon, is totally into her.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does our Sun's illness have anything to do with SCP-1682 (Solar Parasite)? Because if it does then that is a neat little Easter egg. A way to cross link without being so obvious that it spoils the fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a great trippy concept and I still upvoted it, but I can't possibly see how it's classified "Safe" when other people/groups have access to it and can physically damage the stars' avatars with literal cosmic repercussions. Especially since our own sun is one of the dancers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Our sun has a southern accent?
Fuckin' rednecks are everywhere these days!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Soooo its basically a strip-club with stars instead of humans? I can dig it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kinda liked it — it was an original enough concept. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is indeed. Wasn't sure anyone would catch that. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So then is the "Bald" part of Sagittarius A's nickname a reference to this? http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/No-hair_theorem

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think part of my problem with the an/a thing was that I'm still unsure which was correct. I'm mostly certain it's "an" though so I'll just commit to that. ;)

And thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In general I liked it - solid descriptions, weird and unknowable without being over-the-top - but I wasn't really feeling intrigued enough to upvote until that last note. Nicely done.

Grammar nitpick: in some places you say "an SCP-2713 instance" and in others you say "a SCP-2713 instance;" I wasn't sure which you'd prefer so I didn't fix it myself.

Really, it's lovely and understated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay you did it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They used to. The one who didn't get wounded is pretty hostile to pretty much any non -1 or -2 instance though. She just curses in Russian a lot. Doesn't even dance anymore, she just flips people off. Which is sorta weird, but some people seem to enjoy it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was initially more than a little unsure how you'd ever pull off this concept, but you've done a bang-up job.

Do the Matveenko Sisters give dances that last 5700 years?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like it. Stars as exotic dancers. I do like how there's reference to the Sun probably being sick or something. I wonder what the coughing means.

+1 because exotic dancers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
By the way, Big, Black, and Bald refers to Saget Darius Alexander the SCP-XXXX-1 instance and Sagittarius A, the black hole at the center of our galaxy.

You're welcome.

I had this idea strike me from seeing someone use the phrase "Exotic Stars" and I couldn't let it go.

Other puns include "Everything doesn't revolve around you" and the fact that Pi-3 can be read "Pie".

Got great feedback on this from several people, and I'd like to thank them for it.

Image is WikiCommons: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Billiardroom.jpg
